title: sarcanthus ridleyi
side_effects: Sarcanthus ridleyi can have side effects if used improperly, such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and gastrointestinal issues due to its toxic compounds. Ingesting the plant can lead to nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, while prolonged exposure to its sap may cause skin rashes and blisters. Some individuals may also experience respiratory problems, such as wheezing and shortness of breath, especially if they have pre-existing conditions like asthma. Overuse or incorrect handling of the plant can exacerbate these side effects and potentially cause more severe health issues.
